compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary

There is no ongoing per-test performance signal in CI: slow tests are invisible until
someone profiles by hand. Add lightweight, always-on per-test timing to the gate so the
slowest tests surface in every run, and (optionally) persist a durations file usable for
future cross-runner sharding (pytest-split). `--durations` and
`pytest-split --store-durations` are both verified working on this suite.

## What

- Add `--durations=25` to the default-suite pytest invocation in `gerrit-verify.yaml`
  and `test.yml` so every gate run prints its 25 slowest tests (setup/call/teardown).
  Confirmed compatible with `-q`, coverage, and xdist.
- OPTIONAL (behind this task): add a scheduled/post-merge step that runs
  `pytest --store-durations --durations-path .test_durations` and uploads `.test_durations`
  as an artifact, so timing data is tracked over time and available if the team later shards
  across runners with pytest-split. Verified: `--store-durations` writes a valid JSON map
  (`{"<nodeid>": <seconds>, …}`).
- `.test_durations` must be gitignored (or artifact-only) so it never leaks into the
  checkout and never trips the repo-leak guard.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--durations=25` added to the default-suite pytest command in
      `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` and `.github/workflows/test.yml`.
- [ ] A gate run's log shows the 25 slowest tests.
- [ ] If a `.test_durations` store step is added, the file is artifact/gitignored (never
      committed, never leaks into the working tree).
- [ ] Change passes CI (`Verified +1`) and lands via Gerrit.
compiled_state.title: Bake per-test performance logging (--durations) into the gate
